title: Quiz: Quantum Clustering
description: Test your understanding of quantum clustering methods

questions.question: How is quantum distance between two data points typically computed?

options.text: By measuring the overlap (fidelity) between encoded quantum states

questions.explanation: Quantum distance is derived from state fidelity: d(x,y) = √(2(1-|⟨ψ_x|ψ_y⟩|²)). Higher fidelity means lower distance.

questions.question: In quantum k-means, what quantum advantage is primarily leveraged?

options.text: Quantum distance calculations and kernel methods

questions.explanation: Quantum k-means leverages quantum distance calculations and kernels to potentially capture more complex similarity structures than Euclidean distance.

questions.question: What is the role of the quantum kernel in spectral clustering?

options.text: To determine the number of clusters
To perform the final k-means step

questions.explanation: The quantum kernel defines similarity between points, which is used to construct the affinity matrix for spectral clustering's graph-based approach.

questions.question: The silhouette score ranges from -1 to 1, where higher values indicate better clustering.
questions.explanation: Silhouette score measures how similar points are to their own cluster vs other clusters. +1 means well-clustered, 0 means overlapping clusters, -1 means misassigned.

questions.question: What does the Davies-Bouldin index measure?

options.text: Ratio of within-cluster scatter to between-cluster separation

questions.explanation: Davies-Bouldin index measures the average ratio of within-cluster scatter to between-cluster distance. Lower values indicate better-separated clusters.

questions.question: In spectral clustering, what mathematical object is analyzed to find cluster structure?
questions.explanation: Spectral clustering analyzes the eigenvectors of the graph Laplacian (derived from the similarity matrix) to find cluster structure in the data.

questions.question: What is 'inertia' in k-means clustering?

options.text: Sum of squared distances from points to their centroids

questions.explanation: Inertia (or within-cluster sum of squares) measures how internally coherent clusters are by summing squared distances from each point to its centroid.

questions.question: Which are advantages of quantum clustering methods? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Can capture non-linear similarities via quantum kernels
Work with implicit high-dimensional feature spaces

questions.explanation: Quantum methods can capture complex similarities (a), offer potential speedups (b), and work with implicit feature spaces (d). They don't guarantee global optima (c).
